title: Home Protection Scheme (HPS)
text:
Home Protection Scheme (HPS) - SupportGoWhere
Home Protection Scheme (HPS)
Share link
Mortgage-reducing insurance that protects you and your loved ones from losing your Housing Development Board (HDB) flat in the event of death, terminal illness, or total permanent disability.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Payout up to the sum assured will be made to HDB or the mortgagee directly
Processing of claim application generally takes 5 working days upon CPF Board's receipt of requested documents
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

For claims under death grounds

HPS benefits will be paid in a lump sum (based on the share of HPS cover) to HDB or the mortgagee directly to settle the outstanding housing loan. 

Any excess claim amounts will be paid to the insured member's CPF Ordinary Account. 

Thereafter, the deceased member’s CPF savings will be distributed to nominees, or next-of-kins depending on whether there is a valid nomination made. 




For claims admitted under medical grounds

Housing instalments (based on the share of HPS cover) may be paid by HPS for a period of up to two years, before a review of insured member's eligibility to claim the remaining insured sum in full is carried out.

Who is this for?

CPF members who own a HDB flat and are HPS policyholders paying their monthly housing instalments using CPF savings or cash are eligible to apply for HPS coverage.

HPS claim payout is made to:

Next-of-kin of HPS policyholder who is deceased, or

HPS policyholder who is certified by a registered medical practitioner under the Medical Registration Act to be suffering from:

Terminal illness: an illness that is likely to result in the death of the insured CPF member within 12 months

Total permanent disability: the inability to take part in any employment permanently, or the total permanent loss of physical function of any of the following:

Both eyes, or

Two limbs, or

One eye and one limb

How to apply?

Apply online for HPS coverage via the CPF website with your Singpass.




Claim on death grounds

If the insured member has an outstanding housing loan, CPF Board will automatically assess the deceased’s claim eligibility upon Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA)'s notification. 

CPF Board will inform the co-owner(s) or the insured's next-of-kin of the outcome and if additional information is required.




Claim on medical grounds

Submit your application to CPF Board directly via the CPF website

Where can I find help?

View more details

CPF Hotline: 1800 227 1188

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Write to us

Visit your nearest branch 
Note: By appointment only

Scheme last updated 10 Mar 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
